CARLISLE. Pa. Nov 23.-Me-sou-
walk walking Fox. Sac and Fox
tribe. is more widely known as Keo-
kuk, Jr.. favorite son of his father,
the great and eloquent old Chief Keo-
kuk. in whose honor the city of Keo-
kuk. Ia. was named, is dead at the
age of so.


He was a man of fine form, over
six feet in height. with a commanding
manner and an eloquent voice. His
word was law among his people.
The Sac and Fox nation belong to
the great Algonkian family. in many
respects the finest of all the tribes.
According to the government reports
they are full-blooded healthy Indians
generally of good habits. Iaw-abiding
and as industrious as COMFORTS with
the dignity of the noble redman. when
he is fortunate enough to receive q
comfortable annuity.
Many of the Indians are opposed tc
education, because they fear, as they
say. that their childrens hearts may
change and become like the white
i mans.


Keoleuk. Jr., was wealthy, having
large herds of cattle and cash an
nuity of $3.50 from the United States
but he always set a good example t.
his tribe, believing in Christianity. in
| dustry and religion.


He sent his young grandson. Rob
ert, a noble-looking boy. to Carlisle
Pa. where he is rapidly being devel
oped into a scholarly young athlete
worthy scion of the Istalwart OLE
| chief,
